STRENGTH.description: Prioritize complete recovery between heavy sets to maximize force production.

HYPERTROPHY.description: Balance recovery with metabolic stress to maximize muscle growth.

ENDURANCE.description: Shorter recovery improves muscular endurance and work capacity.

WEIGHT_LOSS.description: Keep workouts dense while maintaining exercise quality.

GENERAL_FITNESS.description: Moderate recovery balances performance and workout efficiency.

light.notes: Very light preparation sets require minimal recovery.

moderate.notes: Increase recovery slightly as load increases.

heavy.notes: Final warm-up sets should allow adequate recovery before the first working set.

sameMuscleGroup.notes: Transition after recovering breathing and focus.

differentMuscleGroup.notes: Changing movement patterns generally requires less recovery.

heavyCompoundTransition.notes: Take additional time before another demanding compound exercise.

superset.description: Perform paired exercises consecutively before resting.

restPause.description: Brief intra-set recovery extends total repetitions.

clusterSet.description: Short pauses maintain force production during heavy lifting.

strength: Longer rest generally improves force production.
Do not shorten rest simply to increase workout intensity.
hypertrophy: Rest long enough to maintain training quality.
endurance: Shorter rest intentionally increases cardiovascular demand.
Exercise technique should remain consistent despite fatigue.
explosiveTraining: Every repetition should be performed explosively.
Terminate the set if movement speed noticeably decreases.
compoundExercises: Large muscle groups require longer recovery.
isolationExercises: Smaller muscle groups recover more quickly.
Shorter rest is acceptable if technique remains controlled.

llmGuidelines.explanationTemplate: Recommended rest periods are based on the training goal, exercise type and desired physiological adaptation rather than a fixed value.
